atoms.semantic_claim: The old divergence introduced a standalone `ritual-scheduling` crate as the scheduling service boundary.

destinations.evidence: The v2.2 workspace has no `crates/scheduling` member; it has `crates/ritual-scheduled-verification` and `crates/ritual-scheduled-block-building` instead.
Destination crate owns Scheduler parent-state decoding and verification.
Destination crate owns scheduled transaction synthesis and block-building helpers.

atoms.equivalence_argument: Non-exact ownership split: the old crate manifest is intentionally not present, while behavior-bearing scheduled code is split into verification and block-building crates. This proves broad ownership migration, not complete API compatibility for any stale `ritual-scheduling` consumers.
atoms.test_strategy: workspace metadata plus scheduled package compile
atoms.negative_test_candidate: A stale downstream consumer depending on `ritual-scheduling` should fail to compile unless it was ported to the split v2 crates.
atoms.notes: High-risk only because this is a public crate-boundary change; no source repository was modified.

adversarial_reviews.reason: Verify no live v2.2 consumer still requires the old `ritual-scheduling` crate boundary.

summary: The old `ritual-scheduling` manifest is not exact-preserved; v2.2 splits scheduled behavior across dedicated verification and block-building crates.
notes: Queue entry verified as file 414: A	crates/scheduling/Cargo.toml.
Source hunk begins at divergence diff line 81155.
Destination workspace intentionally has no `crates/scheduling` path.
